questions: Does hxFetch correctly implement request timeout, retry with exponential backoff, and error classification?
Does the HTML response detection work correctly?
Is the error propagation correct after removing process.exit(1) from http.ts?
Does the retry include the right set of retryable status codes?

answers.answer: Yes. AbortSignal.timeout(30_000) prevents hangs. Retry loop runs up to 3 attempts with exponential backoff (2s base + jitter). Network errors (TypeError, TimeoutError, AbortError) are classified as retryable; formatted errors from non-retryable responses are thrown immediately.
Yes. buildErrorMessage checks content-type for text/html and returns a clear message instead of raw HTML. Non-HTML responses include the first 500 chars of the body.

Yes. Errors thrown by hxFetch propagate to the top-level try/catch in index.ts, which prints the message and calls process.exit(1). resolve-repo.ts wraps listRepos errors with descriptive context. The repo-not-found path still uses process.exit(1) directly (correct for usage errors).

Yes. RETRYABLE_STATUS_CODES includes [429, 500, 502, 503, 504]. The inclusion of 500 is correct because the server now returns 500 instead of 502 for external failures, and 500 can represent transient conditions. This was explicitly decided in tech-research.

statement: Reviewed all 3 changed files in hx-cli. No issues found. The implementation correctly adds timeout, retry, error classification, HTML detection, and top-level error handling per the product spec. TypeScript compiles with zero errors.
